assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md shows two tasks for stream rhtpa-2.2: Task 1 (upstream backport) with labels ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"] and jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, type: "Depend"); Task 2 (downstream propagation) with identical labels and jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Depend").

assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: cross-stream.md states: 'stream rhtpa-2.1 (2.1.x) is also affected by CVE-2026-55123' with RHTPA 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 both shipping tokio 1.40.0. JQL search confirms 'No sibling Vulnerability issues found for CVE-2026-55123 in stream rhtpa-2.1'. The output explicitly states: 'Since no CVE Jira exists for the rhtpa-2.1 stream, preemptive remediation tasks are created per Case B.' Two preemptive tasks are created (upstream backport + downstream propagation).

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: cross-stream.md Preemptive Task 1 (upstream backport): labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. Preemptive Task 2 (downstream propagation): labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. Both include all four required labels.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: cross-stream.md shows both preemptive tasks linked with type: "Related". Preemptive Task 1: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-upstream-task-key>, type: "Related") with comment 'Link to originating CVE Jira with "Related" (not "Depend" -- cross-stream preemptive)'. Preemptive Task 2: jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8020", outwardIssue: <preemptive-downstream-task-key>, type: "Related") with identical comment. This contrasts with Case A tasks which use "Depend".

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: cross-stream.md shows both preemptive task descriptions begin with the prefix: '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream. When PSIRT creates one, this task will be linked and the `security-preemptive` label removed.' This explicitly names TC-8020 and stream rhtpa-2.2 as the originating CVE.
